key_insight: PriEco-DRL jointly learns electric-bus eco-driving and transit-priority signal timing to cut energy use while preserving schedule reliability, comfort, and network performance.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a practical control strategy for electrified bus corridors, enabling operators to tune the energy–time trade-off without sacrificing transit priority.
method: Priority-weighted max-pressure adaptive signal control allocates green time using occupancy-aware pressure.
A DRL vehicle controller adapts bus longitudinal speed to uncertain, evolving signal cues.
Centralized training with decentralized execution and parameter sharing lets one agent learn from multiple buses/routes using local observations.
evidence_signal: In real-world corridor experiments, the method outperformed fixed-time, actuated, and rule-based baselines, reducing energy use while maintaining efficiency and transit priority.
watch_item: Performance depends on careful reward weighting and reliable local signal-state estimation, so transfer to other corridors may require retuning and validation.
paper_title: PriEco-DRL: Joint Optimization of Electric-Bus Eco-Driving and Transit-Priority Adaptive Signals via Deep Reinforcement Learning
